mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a healthy labor market, supporting consumer confidence and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% in Sep 2017 is historically low
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with UNRATE below 5% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS), exceeding a certain threshold, indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where the RSAFS growth trigger is met over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve raises interest rates (FEDFUNDS) while unemployment (UNRATE) is already low, it signals an aggressive tightening stance to curb inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 3 months AND UNRATE < 5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.15% and UNRATE at 4.2% suggests potential for further tightening
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the combined signal shows no statistically significant predictive power (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates increasing manufacturing activity and business investment, which is a strong positive for corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery regimes, positive INDPRO growth does not lead to significantly positive SPX 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) above a certain level indicates strong demand in the housing sector, which has broad positive spillover effects on related industries and consumer wealth, boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1200 (thousands of units) AND 3-month average growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1180.0 in Aug 2017 is near the trigger
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where the HOUST trigger is met over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and often leads to tighter monetary policy, all of which are detrimental to corporate profitability and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 246.373 in Sep 2017, indicating moderate inflation
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a statistically significant positive correlation with CPIAUCSL 12-month growth > 2.5% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust job growth (PAYEMS) combined with strong GDP growth (GDPC1) signifies a healthy and expanding economy, leading to increased consumer and business confidence, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 150k AND GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146659.0 in Sep 2017, GDPC1 at 17031.1 in Q2 2017
failure_condition: The combination of strong PAYEMS and GDPC1 growth does not consistently predict positive SPX 60-day returns, or the correlation is not statistically significant (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a certain level, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing borrowing costs for companies and consumers, which typically leads to a slowdown in economic activity and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.15% in Sep 2017, approaching potential trigger
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5% over a 5-year period.
